response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a highly experienced senior engineer with 15+ years of diverse software development experience, which makes him significantly overqualified for this entry-level Java developer role. More critically, his German is self-assessed as only 'Basic,' which is a hard requirement for client-facing consulting work in the German insurance sector. While he has Java exposure and strong agile practices, his primary expertise lies in TypeScript and Node.js, and he has no insurance sector background.
strengths: Holds a Computer Science degree and has demonstrated strong software craftsmanship practices including TDD, CI/CD, and agile methodologies across multiple roles
Java experience is present in prior roles (Altom Consulting: robotic arms, mobile device orchestration) alongside broad OOP background across many languages
Proven ability to adapt across diverse domains and technology stacks, demonstrating high learning agility
concerns: Massively overqualified — 15+ years of experience including Staff and Senior engineer roles raises serious concerns about retention, motivation, and fit for an entry-level position
German proficiency is self-assessed as only 'Basic,' which is a hard disqualifier for client-facing insurance consulting in Germany where strong German communication is explicitly required
No insurance sector experience and primary stack is TypeScript/Node.js rather than Java, which is the core technology for this role
